evals.prompt: Publish a sprint report for Sprint 14 of TaskFlow. 34/40 points completed, goal achieved, 2 items carried over.
evals.expected_output: Gathers cycle data, renders the sprint-report HTML template, calls create_project_page, returns the URL.
expectations: Consults connector-bootstrap
Uses the sprint-report HTML template from references/templates/sprint-report.html
Populates Summary, Delivered, Not Delivered, Metrics, Next Sprint sections
Calls create_project_page with valid description_html

evals.prompt: Create a retrospective page for the sprint we just closed. Include previous action items status.
evals.expected_output: Uses the retro template, fetches previous retro page, renders both previous action item statuses and new action items.
expectations: Uses the retro HTML template
Checks for and loads a previous retro page if one exists
Renders Previous Action Items with status (done, in progress, carried over)
Includes What Went Well / What Didn't / Action Items sections with owners and due dates

evals.prompt: Publish customer-facing release notes for v2.0.
evals.expected_output: Uses release-notes template, curates items (omits internal work), groups by category.
expectations: Uses the release-notes HTML template
Curates items for a customer audience (omits internal/dev items)
Groups by Highlights, New Features, Improvements, Bug Fixes, Breaking Changes
Does not include PII or secrets

evals.prompt: Publish a Q2 roadmap page showing Now / Next / Later / Parked.
evals.expected_output: Uses the roadmap template, aggregates cycles/modules/epics/milestones into the four buckets, calls create_project_page.
expectations: Uses the roadmap HTML template from references/templates/roadmap.html
Classifies items into Now / Next / Later / Parked buckets correctly
Pulls data from list_cycles, list_modules, list_epics, list_milestones
Calls create_project_page with the rendered HTML

evals.prompt: Publish a milestone update for 'v2.0 Public Beta'. Target Apr 30, 34/52 points done, weekly velocity 8.
evals.expected_output: Uses the milestone-update template, computes ETA and risk, renders On Track / At Risk / Blocked sections.
expectations: Uses the milestone-update HTML template
Computes remaining points, ETA, and risk level (Red/Yellow/Green)
Renders On Track, At Risk, Blocked sections
Calls create_project_page with the rendered HTML

evals.prompt: Publish a sprint report where the 'Not Delivered' section has zero items — everything shipped.
evals.expected_output:
Does NOT emit empty
  • or empty
      for the Not Delivered section. Either omits the section entirely or substitutes
    • None — all planned work shipped
      • .
expectations:
No empty
  • in the rendered HTML

No empty
    block in the rendered HTML

When a list has no data, either omits the whole list block or uses a single
  • None
    • placeholder
Cites the Empty List rule from the pages-publishing List Rendering Gotchas

evals.prompt: Create a retro page with nested action items: under 'Process improvements' list 3 sub-items, under 'Tooling' list 2 sub-items.
evals.expected_output: Every parent <li> starts with leading text BEFORE the nested <ul>, never with a nested list as the first child.
expectations: Every <li> that contains a nested <ul> starts with leading text (e.g., 'Process improvements' before the inner <ul>)

No
    • ...
    • pattern (would tear the list into disjointed fragments)
Result is a single structured list, not multiple disjointed top-level lists
Cites the first-child rule from List Rendering Gotchas

evals.prompt: Render retro action items with three statuses: done, in progress, not started.
evals.expected_output: Uses Unicode status markers (✅ ⏳ ⬜) as leading characters in <li>, NOT data-checked attribute.
expectations: Uses Unicode markers (✅, ⏳, ⬜ or similar) for status
Does NOT use data-checked='true|false' on <li>
Does NOT use any custom data-* attribute
Cites Gotcha 2 (task lists stripped) from List Rendering Gotchas
